title: Diplomatic protocol and AI-assisted public-sector management
summary: Candidate topic linking digital protocol, artificial intelligence, public-sector transformation and institutional communication.
research_angle: How AI changes protocol workflows, verification, ceremonial planning and public-sector institutional trust.
verification: Manual literature verification required through Academic Search Hub and editorial screening.

title: Women in diplomacy and ceremonial visibility
summary: Candidate topic on the protocol dimension of representation, seating, speaking order, delegation leadership and signature visibility.
research_angle: Gender representation as a measurable protocol and diplomatic visibility issue.
verification: Requires source triangulation and journal editorial review.

title: Crisis communication as protocol architecture
summary: Candidate topic treating crisis communication not only as messaging but as institutional order, sequence and authority signal.
research_angle: How protocol structures shape credibility, authority and timing in public crisis response.
verification: Manual verification and source collection required.

title: Security conferences as ceremonial diplomacy
summary: Candidate topic on the ceremonial order, speaker sequence, visibility and partnership signaling in security conferences.
research_angle: Protocol-as-signal in security dialogue, defence diplomacy and regional trust-building.
verification: Requires event-source verification and conflict-of-interest check.

title: Communicology of institutional reputation in digital academies
summary: Candidate topic on the reputation logic of independent digital academies, disclosure, disclaimers and trust-building.
research_angle: How transparent status language, fair-access models and AI ethics statements build institutional credibility.
verification: Requires comparative sources and editorial screening.
